perturbation.original_text: 1. DEFINITIONS In this Agreement, the following expressions shall have the following meanings: 1.1 'Purpose' shall mean any discussions and negotiations between or within the parties concerning or in connection with the establish ment of a business relationship between the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. DEFINITIONS In this Agreement, the following expressions shall have the following meanings: 1.1 'Purpose' shall mean strictly limited to the initial assessment of potential business synergies and expressly excludes any subsequent operational collaborations, joint ventures, or any other form of partnership not explicitly detailed in writing and signed by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Purpose' is broad, encompassing any discussions related to a business relationship. The modified definition narrows it significantly, limiting it to the initial assessment phase only. This change contradicts the wider interpretation implied elsewhere in the document, especially concerning ongoing obligations and use of confidential information beyond initial assessment. This creates uncertainty about the scope of permitted activities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 In consideration of the mutual exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information, each party undertakes in relation to the other party's Confidential Information: (a) to maintain the same in confidence and to use it only for the Purpose and for no other purpose and in particular, but without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing: (i) not to make any commercial use thereof; (ii) not to use the same for the benefit of itself or of any third (3rd) party o ther than pursuant to a further agreement with the other party; and (iii) not to use the same for the purpose of guiding or conducting a search of any information, materials or sources, whether or not available to the public, for any purpose whatsoever, including without limitation, for the purpose of demonstrating that any information falls within one (1) of the exceptions in Clause 1;
perturbation.justification: The modified text significantly narrows the definition of 'Purpose' to only include the initial assessment of business synergies, explicitly excluding subsequent collaborations or partnerships. This contradicts section 2.1, which states that confidential information can be used 'only for the Purpose'. The original broad definition of 'Purpose' in the original text allowed for the use of confidential information in a wider range of business relationship discussions. The modified definition restricts this use, creating a conflict regarding the scope of permissible activities with confidential information, making the two sections incompatible.

perturbation.original_text: 2.3 Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Recipient shall be entitled to make any disclosure required by law of the other party's Confidential Information, but shall give the other party not less than two (2) business days' notice of such disclo sure and shall consult with the Discloser prior to such disclosure with a view to avoiding such disclosure if legally possible.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3 Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Recipient shall be entitled to immediately make any disclosure required by law of the other party's Confidential Information, and will not be required to notify or consult with the Discloser prior to such disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause requires the Recipient to give two business days' notice and consult with the Discloser before disclosing confidential information required by law. The modified clause removes the notice and consultation requirement, allowing immediate disclosure without informing the Discloser. This contradicts the purpose of maintaining confidentiality as much as possible, creates legal uncertainty, and makes it difficult to enforce the NDA effectively.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 In consideration of the mutual exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information, each party undertakes in relation to the other party's Confidential Information: (a) to maintain the same in confidence and to use it only for the Purpose and for no other purpose and in particular, but without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing: (i) not to make any commercial use thereof; (ii) not to use the same for the benefit of itself or of any third (3rd) party o ther than pursuant to a further agreement with the other party; and (iii) not to use the same for the purpose of guiding or conducting a search of any information, materials or sources, whether or not available to the public, for any purpose whatsoever, including without limitation, for the purpose of demonstrating that any information falls within one (1) of the exceptions in Clause 1;
perturbation.justification: The original clause 2.3 requires the Recipient to give two business days' notice and consult with the Discloser before disclosing confidential information required by law. The modified clause removes this notice and consultation requirement, allowing immediate disclosure without informing the Discloser. This directly contradicts clause 2.1, which requires each party to maintain the other's Confidential Information in confidence. By removing the notice and consultation, the Recipient can disclose the Discloser's confidential information immediately, undermining the obligation to maintain confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: 3. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION Each party shall: (a) within one (1) month of completion of the purpose or receipt of a written request from the other party, return to the other party all documents and materials (and all copies thereof) containing the other party's Confidential Information and certify in writing to the AHPL Non-Disclosure Agreement 6 NDA_ 01012014 other party that it has complied with the requiremen ts of this sub - clause; and (b) notwithstanding the completion of the Purpose or return of the documents and materials as aforesaid, continue to be bound by the undertakings set out in Clause 2.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION Each party is under no obligation to return the other party's information at any time. AHPL Non-Disclosure Agreement 6 NDA_ 01012014
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates the return of confidential information within one month of purpose completion or written request. The modified clause states that there is no obligation to return the other party's information at any time. This is a direct contradiction that undermines the protection of confidential information and creates uncertainty regarding the rights of the Discloser, given AHPL's data, described in section 4.1, shall remain at all times the property of AHPL.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. DATA PROTECTION 4.1 Any data provided by AHPL to the Comp any and used by the Company directly or directly in the performance of this Agreement shall remain at all times the property of AHPL . It shall be identified, clearly marked and recorded as such by the Company on all media and in all documentation.
perturbation.justification: The original text in clause 3 mandates the return of confidential information. The changed text in clause 3 states that there is no obligation to return the other party's information at any time. This directly contradicts section 4.1, which states that data provided by AHPL remains the property of AHPL, implying an obligation of return or protection. Therefore, the modification creates a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11. TERMINATION This Agreement shall continue to be in force from the date hereof until terminated by mutual consent or by either party giving to the other not AHPL Non-Disclosure Agreement 9 NDA_ 01012014 less than thirty (30) days’ prior written notice. The provision of Clauses 1 to 8, 11 to 15 and 17 to 21 shall survive any such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. TERMINATION This Agreement shall continue to be in force from the date hereof. Termination may only occur at the sole discretion of the AHPL, and can occur without prior notification. The provision of Clauses 1 to 8, 11 to 15 and 17 to 21 shall survive any such termination.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause allows either party to terminate the agreement with 30 days' notice. The modified clause states that termination can only occur at the sole discretion of AHPL without prior notification. This contradicts the reciprocity of the original termination clause, and is contradictory to AHPL's obligation to comply with the Agreement, implying that AHPL could be bind by the agreement while the counterparty isn't.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY In carrying out their respective obligations under this Agreement, the Company and AHPL shall comply with all laws and regulations applicable thereto but save for wilful acts, default or gross negligence on their respective parts, neither party shall be liable to the other party for any indirect, incidental, special, punitive or consequential damages however caused, including any loss of profit s or business interruption costs and under any theory of liability, including but not limited to contract, strict liability and negligence; whether or not the other party has been advised of the possibility of such damage.
perturbation.justification: The original clause states that either party can terminate the agreement with 30 days' written notice. However, the changed clause states that termination can only occur at AHPL's sole discretion without prior notification. This directly contradicts the principle of mutual obligation outlined in Clause 9, which states that both the Company and AHPL have respective obligations and liabilities under the agreement. By giving AHPL unilateral termination power, the modification undermines the balance of obligations and contradicts AHPL's responsibility mentioned in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: 20. GOVERNING LAW 20.1 This Agreement shall be deemed to be made in Singapore, subject to, governed by and construed in a ll respects in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Singapore for every intent and purpose. 20.2 The parties hereby agree to submit irrevocably to the non -exclusive jurisdiction of the Courts of the Republic of Singapore to settle any and all dispu tes in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 20. GOVERNING LAW 20.1 This Agreement shall be deemed to be made in Singapore, subject to, governed by and construed in a ll respects in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Singapore for every intent and purpose. 20.2 The parties hereby agree that any and all disputes in connection with this Agreement will be subject to binding arbitration in Switzerland, and will not have access to the Courts of the Republic of Singapore.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies that disputes will be resolved in the courts of Singapore. The modified clause states that all disputes will be subject to binding arbitration in Switzerland, explicitly denying access to Singapore courts. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the dispute resolution mechanism and location, creating uncertainty about which legal system governs dispute resolution. The changed text also contradicts section 15.3, by disallowing court proceedings.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15.3 In the event that mediation is unsuccessful, the dispute shall be resolved either by reference to arbitration or by court proceedings as elected by either party, by way of a written notice to the other party, which shall state the specific dispute to be resolved and the nature of such dispute.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies Singapore courts for dispute resolution, while the changed text mandates binding arbitration in Switzerland, explicitly denying access to Singapore courts. This creates a direct conflict with section 15.3, which allows either arbitration or court proceedings at either party's election. The changed text removes the option of court proceedings, creating a clear and direct contradiction.
